I nearly forgot, to hire an e-bike: $70 per bike for 4 hrs. For us 3 that would have been $210 for a trip into town and back which is a real shame because it is within beautiful riding distance to town. No option to hire a normal bike.

Overall a strange experience.

If l could give zero l would. Do not stay at this park. We have travelled extensively in our caravan staying in all kinds of places from free camping, showgrounds and caravan parks and this is the worst place we have ever stayed. There's too many things to list. The sites are dirt patches that turn to mud lakes with any rain. We had requested a large site due to size of our van and were swapped to a site we didn't fit on, staff offered one alternate site which we only just fit on and then had no where to park our car. We booked 10months ago and people that only stayed one night in tiny campervans were given the large sites? Amentities were disgusting, pool not clean, cabanas broken and so dirty you couldn't sit on them. We didnt see any groundsmen in the 5 days we were there. We were so happy to leave today. I've never done a review on a caravan park before until now. I don't want other ppls stay at Byron ruined by staying here. Other parks in town and other side of town. Will be back to Byron but never staying here again.
We had a great stay here. Lots included in your park rate. Our daughter had a ball. Green grass - great for our caravan. Thank you.

After arriving we were directed to powered site 354(?) where we proceeded to set up our van, when we were almost done, just had to put the grey water hose into the pit provided, we saw that someone had emptied their toilet in it. We went to the office and explained the situation, where we were offered another site and told that they had had 40ml of rain a couple of days earlier, what this has to do with human waste and filth I have no idea. Also the site it’s self should have been closed off and the adjoining sites should have been notified and the site it’s self should have been closed for maintenance because of the amount of water still sitting on it considering we paid $75 for it. Driving past the next morning we noticed that the pit had not been cleaned or barricaded off considering that it had human waste in it, this being a BioHazard.  We were traveling with family who were staying in a cabin, 122, which at full rate was $175 for the night, the curtain was falling off the track, the whole building is on a lean, needs restumping, the door into the bathroom was incredibly hard to close/open, and all round the cabin was dirty. Having stayed in many caravan parks, including Discovery Parks, I expected a much cleaner and more responsive attitude from the staff, we will never be staying at Discovery Parks in Byron Bay again and will be letting family and friends know what we experienced.

Top quality facilities, amenities, and grounds,

But the powered tent sites suffer from bad road noise from being right next to Ewingsdale Rd. After big rain drainage was average too. Pick a different type of site and I think you'd be golden.

Location is decent, with walkable food options nearby. Or alternatively, location is great if you love Bunnings!

A great location with just a 2 minute drive into town. However the caravan sites are a disgrace. Water logged, mud pits. Yes I understand you can’t help the rain, but you can also put down concrete slabs with drainage to help this. Not a pleasant stay but could easily be fixed!
We love Byron but would love elsewhere next time!

I had a one bedroom cabin in the park for three nights.  It was quite old but still comfortable.  The double bed was very comfortable and the air conditioning was very nice.  I was pleased with the blinds which assured my privacy and also allowed me to sleep in.  Nice big fridge, great kitchen facilities including a microwave oven.  All the places we wanted to visit were in close proximity.
The park had the best pools, camp kitchen and organised pizza night, a bbq, and yoga for guests.

Ok camp sites. Great facilities and kept clean twice a day during our stay. However more should be done to curb noise after 10pm. We had to tell a couple of nights in a row a camp site behind us that it is late and keep the noise down. Gates not always closed all the time even at nighttime. What is the point of an access code?!  Better signage for the campsites.
Cool water park, serviceable cabin, exorbitant pricing - but that is seemingly on point for Byron.
The lady speaking in the phone call is absolutely  rude don't want to listen clients queries.
Beautiful stress free park

I’ve had such an amazing experience at these amazing place!
Really good location and all the amenities are amazing!

I would just like to be able to buy water from the machine using cash or tap my card, because the only way was putting my card details on a website which I don’t feel comfortable!

Everything else was amazing!!
Definitely going back

Had a great stay at the discovery park in Byron Bay.  Although was disappointed with the balcony (it was more of a ledge).   You couldn’t sit outside on balcony, but the doors did open up.  We had frog mouth tawny owls outside our cabin and it was overlooking a lake, so very pretty.  Too far to walk into Byron so had to drive.

Avoid Camping, the tent sites are parallel to the main road into Byron so you'll hear every vehicle on its way past.
Tent sites are about 100m from the nearest toilet and it's pitch black at night walking past the permanent residence so not ideal for female customers.
The main gate was open at 11 o'clock at night which was alarming...
Other than that it was a lovely spot and there are families of ducks wandering around which we enjoyed looking at. It would be a great spot for kids as there is a little water park and jumping cushion.
Lieu sympathique, sanitaires commun tres propre, loué pour 6 mais réellement pour 4,

Huge park and very well kept. Lots of accommodation options for all budgets. We stayed in a lower price point cabin. The cabin was older style but clean and comfortable. There is a very nice amenities block & camp kitchen which is accessible for all. Would absolutely return and try one of the  more expensive cabins. Highly recommended. As a plus search out the local shopping centre and Sunday Sustainable Bakery....best pastries ever.

We spent one night here.
I found the staff not to be helpful at all. A lady asked for the code for the bathroom they said we don't know. The stuff on Front desk have noise rings Not A Good Look.The Cabin it self was very clean. where you stay its all lovely however where the tenants live in this same park its run down.

We stayed at Discovery Parks - Byron Bay in a Deluxe Cabin for two nights from the 13th & 14th July 2024, as we were down for a wedding party.

Most importantly I want to mention Monica Machin, the Front Office Manager, who displayed Excellent Customer Service Skills when faced with a couple of minor issues that I had raised with her.
Hence I had no hesitation in giving Monica a well earned FIVE STAR Rating.

As a result we have no hesitation in recommending Discovery Parks Byron Bay, when you decide your next vacation in Byron Bay, as we certainly will.
Poor cleaning cabin. Not enough blankets.
시설 캐빈안은 너무나 예쁜 인테리어지만어디서 알을 깟는지 갓 태어난 새끼 바퀴벌레들이 계속 나왔다그리고 칼이 닳아 음식이 썰리지 않았고 가위도 없었다하지만 전체적으로는 만족합니다
Clean and cosy cabins, able to cook in the cabin. Spaces are a little tight and theres hardly internet connection, but overall a nice and pleasant stay.

The park was dead quiet in School holidays. They cancelled the kids club activities which was a bad choice with wet weather. The cabin we stayed in didn’t have an outdoor deck or sitting area. So glad they finally have a heated pool.
Amazing staff with great facilities and the location is one of the best in the area we at East Coast Baby Hire proudly hire and deliver all baby and holiday items to this amazing park.

I stayed 1 night on a powered caravan site in June 2024, the site was very waterlogged and covered over with sand. It was a mess to try get in and out the caravan. Every camper around me also complained how bad it was. It’s a simple fix, poor concrete pads and you will have a 5 star caravan site. Hope to return one day and the problem is fixed. Otherwise a beautiful park with all the amenities for a family holiday!

We only stayed 2 nights in a superior lake cabin as we are travelling around Oz. Big and comfortable really enjoyed our stay. The only issue I have which is the same in all the Discovery Packs is the terrible wifi might as well not have it as it is so weak it is nearly non existent. I couldn’t join so used up a lot of mobile data. If you need wifi to do some work no point staying in any Discovery Park.
Very expensive for what you get muddy grounds unable to use awning as you could not sit outside because of the grounds. Toilet and shower block not clean in the caravan area.

This park may be great in sunny weather but if you happen to be camping here when it’s raining you’ll have a terrible stay. It rained our entire stay which can’t be helped. The issue is that there is very poor drainage around the sites, the sites themselves are very poorly maintained (big ditches on the middle of the sites that fill with water), and there were no slab sites at all. By day 2 we were surrounded by mud. Our poor neighbours were stepping out into a huge puddle that covered at least half their site. the amenities are also very poorly designed. The drainage in the showers is so bad that the shower water flows all through the bathroom. So if you just walk in to go to the toilet you have to walk through someone else’s shower water. Would not recommend this place at all. 2 stars were for the kids jumping pillow and pool which did look good.
Loved our stay here. Affordable accomodation on the beach front, doesn’t get much better. Extended our trip by a week because we loved it so much. Thanks!

When we arrived the staff member on reception was curt and offered no information about the park, just gave us a map. The site we were allocated to was so bogged we didn't attempt to get on it. I rang reception who said yes you can move, find your own spot. The whole area was ankle deep in water and mud.
I realise no one can predict inclement weather but at least get off your butt and see which sites are unusable to save your paying customers the job of doing it themselves.
Also we didn't use the kitchens or kitchen equipment. The ovens/stoves smelt of old oil. The freezer was 1/2 full of out of date food and the fridge had mouldy food in it. To say we were disappointed is an understatement. We left one night early. On a positive note we loved Byron Bay!

We stayed for 3 nights. Our initial thoughts on our cabin were that it was dirty,with marks on the walls & curtains. On our first night, we noticed the light switch in the bathroom was pushed into the wall, so unable to use. It took the management 2 days to repair the switch, so we had showers in the dark. They did offer us another cabin however, this was unsuitable for us. The team on the front desk were pleasant to deal with & the park is in a good location just that little bit away from the  noise of town.

Location, while close to BB CBD, not relieved in price for being away from it, in revived swamp. Watch the mozzies. Rooms can only speak to the deluxe tents, which have mould problems due to the constant rain they've had recently. Bowls you over when you walk in. Service, room felt mucky, reception staff will get to you when they can but are happy to chat to each other until their conversation is over before even acknowledging that you have entered the room, and unapologetic for it. The owner was a perfect host, met him later, and he was what a host should be. Bless him.

The cabins are run down and dodgy. There was have drunk popper juice in the fridge, the screens were coming off the windows, I dare not open it! Mould around the windows too. The window sill near the bunk bed wasn’t clean, dusty and looks like black mould forming. Sliding door track full of dirt. Bed is like a really bad hospital bed. Need a high vehicle to park into allocated parking. Would think for $250 a night it would be a bit less run down. These cabins are not like the pictures I saw when I booked. Very disappointing. Glad I have a bed, fridge and microwave. The only thing going for it. I was in number 98.

Cabins are expensive, but everything in Byron Bay is expensive, which I think is very over rated . Park has clean amenities and is great for families. Byron is a beautiful spot, but there are lots of beautiful spots along that coast which are as good or better.
A great experience overall, wish we had time to say longer!

Tja, wenn man ein Quasimonopol hat in Byron Bay, kann man nicht mehr verlangen.

Positiv:
- schöne Poolanlage
- relativ neue WC/Duschen & Küche

Negativ:
- 129 Dollar/2 Nächte Powerd für einen Schlammplatz ist doch schon viel, zumal der Platz fast leer ist. Aber ja: sie haben das Monopol
- Kein W-Lan und das was es gibt ist so schlecht, dass man gerade so Nachrichten schreiben kann. Hey wir haben 2024!!!
- sehr viele Mücken, so dass man nicht draussen sitzen kann. Ausser man sprayt sich selber zu tode 🤪

We arrived at the caravan park during the opening hours and were "greeted" by a receptionist telling us to check the sms we received to check in (there was no other customer at the reception). We felt quite unwelcomed and didn't want to bother the receptionist more so we didn't feel like asking about the activities we can do in the area.
The park itself was good, the amenities were nice and clean.
Great park, great service, 10/10!
Brilliant for families or just a quick getaway, great environment, quite a large facility. Was very impressed, no wonder it is so popular

Check in was only 2pm for campsite. We were told to stop making a noise( we were only talking) and go to bed by 10.06pm from the groundsman(How rude). We did a group booking in various locations at the park The kids facilities all close at 5pm even during the school holidays. The kids got kicked off the tennis court because it was closed. Advertised wifi, but nobody could connect. Everything was charged for at discovery Byron bay for usage of the facilities except the pools, which also close at 5pm. Not happy. The cabins wasn't cleaned properly. Wont go back there again.

Honestly the Discovery Park Byron Bay is the worst accommodation we have ever stayed in with lots of travel over the last 12 months
The cabin was so dirty and felt unhygienic
The cleaning is obviously very minimal over many years
The park also resembles a tip site with rubbish and old furniture piled high at bin site's, over grown lawn, there’s no way I would recommend discovery park Byron Bay to a friend or family member
Absolutely enjoyed our stay we will return
Room was infested with cockroaches and spiders. Staff didn’t believe us when we told them. Would not recommend unless you like sleeping with bugs.
The restrooms and showers are not in an clean condition. The claeaning Service cleans the mirrors outside, Looks 10 min on the cellphone an drives away… the showers weren‘t cleaned…
Nice But the washhouses are very Dirty and so wlan.

Rooms in cabins basic but well laid out and clean. Beds, pillows good
Water park for kids terrific.
2 pools, one is marvellous for those with mobility issues as they have a slope & railing for entry into pool.
Kids room a bit tight, bunk beds.

Not maintained or staffed properly and also dont trust the staff in the kids club.

Stayed here 4 nights and wouldn't come back. The park is extremely poorly maintained. The main toilet block was filthy on arrival and not cleaned for 48hrs. Park flooded twice and this was due to the sites being poorly maintained not extreme weather.

My 7 year old went to the kids club and staff said I could leave her there and they would call me if she needed to use toilet or wanted to come back etc. When I went to see how she was going 1hr later, nobody knew where she was.... She had left 15 minutes earlier and gone to our friends site we learnt. What a joke.

The toilets were not cleaned for 48hrs and were absolutely filthy and stunk of urine. When they were cleaned, they were left with no toilet paper, the same dirt and scum which had been present the whole trip

Numerous other faults with laundry and things not operating or functioning.

We had friends staying in cabins and this in my opinion appears to be the focus of management and maybe where they make their money.

Caravan sites are poor, hygiene is lacking and maintenance is non apparent.
Absolutely amazing! We stayed in an over water 6 sleeper, and it was huge. Very clean and comfortable. The kids absolutely loved the water park! Would come back for a longer stay!

Cabins are well equipped.
Pools & water park are great for kids.
Communal laundry washing machines grubby and in need of a good clean.
Real easy checking , good size sites. Could work on better drainage for camp sites
Very nice camp site but staff at reception is absolutely incompetent and unhelpful when it comes to any enquiries. What a shame.. 😕

On our two-month road trip along the east coast, we visited 4 Discovery Parks and many other campsites.
The economy cabin at Byron Bay Discovery Park was by far the worst place on our trip.
We had ant trails in our cabin and ants and spiders in our beds. Also, the cupboards were dirty from previous guests. The mattresses were all worn out. The small terrace and terrace furniture were covered in bird droppings and other dirt. We were glad when we were able to move on.
We can only give points for the water park.
The price-performance ratio was absolutely inadequate compared to the other parks we visited.

However, the other Discovery Parks we visited were very good (e.g. Rope and Narooma).
Beautiful place, but there was no Wifi at all. The people at the reception did not answer to our demands.

The ONLY place we’ve stayed in more than 7 months of travelling the world that didn’t have WiFi, this includes the Himalayas where at 4300m (14,000ft) they managed to have it! Other than that, the deluxe lakeside cabin was nice, comfortable, seemed clean and was mostly quiet. The park seemed to have very good facilities and nice grounds. Getting in and out of the park required patience as there is an electronic gate that everyone seems to have an issue with (basically if you exit without entering your code the gate wont let you back in, so people constantly get stuck). I think there are some fairly simple things this park could do to improve guest experience, and if they did so I’d definitely stay again in future!

I recently stayed at Discovery Parks accomodation Byron Bay in the Deluxe Safari Tent, and unfortunately, my experience was far from satisfactory. After arriving around 8pm and making myself comfortable for bed, I encountered a significant infestation of cockroaches and spiders in my room.
As a result, I was forced to sleep in my car for the majority of the night, barely utilizing the accommodation facilities. When I approached the management for a refund due to the unsanitary conditions, they refused, citing their inability to locate the bugs during the day and arguing that I had slept in the room as the bed was unmade from when I had first arrived and gotten comfortable, prior to noticing the bugs.
This lack of accountability and disregard for customer satisfaction is deeply disappointing. I strongly advise against staying at Discovery Parks accomodation until they address and rectify their pest control issues and adopt a more customer-centric approach to handling such situations.

My husband and daughter went camping here over the weekend and had a fantastic time.

Generous camp site sizes with bathrooms close by.
Brilliant water park facilities which saw my 5 year old spend over 5 hours there Saturday.
Jumping pillow was a huge hit.

Cafe breakfast and coffee (pancakes and eggs benny) were a real hit!!

Location was perfect for what they were chasing with town centre a short drive away.

This is our second time staying here and we will absolutely be back.

For a short stay like us (1 night), I would think it would be alright.

The location itself is quite a distance away from any malls to do groceries.

Rooms are basic and I felt that the cabin spaces seems cramped however with the price we paid, for a night to restover for longer upcoming drive, I think its fine.

Unfortunately we didn't enjoy out stay here. We've been put opposite a large group who walked 20x times across out site & used all power plugs. The traffic from the road is really noisy and some of the permanent resident are strange (shouting in the morning, then speeding away with the car).
There was no soap in the bathroom (one soap empty, other not working) and our laundry smelled worse after washing then before. It has a lot potential but the vibe was a bit weird. Wouldn't recommend staying here.
Steht im nirgendwo und viel zu weit weg von Strand. Abends kann man nicht mehr raus weil überall nur Mücken sind. Gibt auch nirgends Mückenfallen. WLAN ist auch nicht gut bis gar nicht vorhanden.
2nd visit there,and loved it...
We have used discovery parks over the last month as far away as Tasmania.We have stayed here twice before.Its a very good park with excellent accommodation.

We very much enjoyed our stay. They had a nice welcome packet, had the AC already on when we arrived so it felt cool, got an upgrade automatically. There's a water park and pools, guest kitchen, and you can play tennis ($10 to rent equip.) Our cabin had toiletries, plenty of pots/pans/dishes/cups/utensils, stuff to wash dishes. The wifi didn't work much but they mentioned this is something they are currently working on after flooding. There were also some ants here and there but it honestly wasn't much. The porch area was great to dry stuff on their rack, the shower had great pressure and could go really hot or cold. Keep in mind there's no housekeeping though. Overall, a really great stay (and even more perfect if you have a car as it's a mile from town, which we did the walk each day anyways.)

We had a great stay me and family! Big parcels and super clean and new amenities, park has all you need for great holidays. Special mention to one of the employees Elsa, she was super nice, she showed us the camp itself, she recommend us places to eat and visit in byron, she was really professional and lovely with us. We’ll definitely come
back. :)

Beautiful camp site with great kids pool area with slides and jumping mat.
Close to main street Byron bay

Great staff 👍
Nice place
자연속의 캠핑장. 숙소에 식기세척기까지 있어서 좋음
十分成熟的motel, 房內乾淨整齊，設施齊全，家庭度假，朋友出遊，方便快捷
For a 2 night stay we had a nearby fire alarm going off until the am the day we arrived & then our own alarm popping off in the early hours of the morning not ideal

This place was really nice and the free yoga lesson was awesome. Thank a lot 😁
(No full equipment to cook in the room, better if you bring your fried-pan. You Can manage something anyway :))
Fantastic thanks for having us

This park was perfect for families! We drove down from Melbourne and had the best experience there. The pools and water park was amazing and cleaned daily and our kids had the best time! There is also free Yoga and kids club which was great for all!
The Cafe at the front had delicious food and coffee.
Our deluxe lake view cabin was lovely and clean and in a great spot. We would definitely come back, overall a fantastic experience!

I was pleasantly surprised with this park. We ended up staying an extra night because we loved it so much. 2 pools, large caravan sites, kids' activities, and a great water park. A bit out of the way of the city centre which is good in my opinion. We will be back
Loved the vibe of the cafe for breakfast

Service:
Man sieht permanent Servicepersonal herumlaufen, welches aufräumt, putzt oder andere Tätigkeiten durchführen.
Auch zu späteren Stunden sind alle sehr freundlich und versuchen ihren Möglichkeiten entsprechend zu helfen
5/5*

Preis:
An sich etwas teurer als die meisten Kampingplätze. (100AUT$ pro Nacht auf powered motorhome Site)
Wenn man sich allerdings die Anlage mit all den Angeboten anschaut erscheint es wiederum sehr fair!
Waschen 4$
5/5*

Sauberkeit:
Ich habe selten einen so sauberen Park gesehen! Liegt vermutlich daran, dass mehrfach am Tag alles geputzt wird.
Also wirklich super Mega sauber!
5/5*

Ausstattung:
Es war alles in bestem Zustand und sah recht neu aus. Die Sanitären Anlagen waren top.
Auch die Küche und die vielen BBQ Plätze waren immer super sauber und in erstklassigem Zustand.
5/5*

Fazit:
Wenn man ein paar Tage einfach mal entspannen möchte ohne groß umher zu reisen, wer nach einigen Tagen der längeren Fahrt einfach mal die Füße hochlegen will und die Kinder draußen ohne große sorge herumhüpfen lassen möchte ohne dass ihnen langweilig wird, dann ist Mann hier goldrichtig
5/5*****
We camped near road, so bit noisy and a bit of walk to the amenities. The amenities block always smelt bad, not sure why, but clean good showers. Good things for the kids.

The location of this park is great and so were the pools/waterpark but quite disappointed with the cleanliness of the bathrooms particularly during school holiday period more then 2 daily cleans I would have thought (based on the cleaning sign off sheet).

Our site was damp (not the parks fault but our neighbouring van), and quite annoying they close the kitchens overnight (especially you need to make an infant a bottle at 1am). Additionally saw at least 3 rats in the 2 nights we stayed there. Wouldn’t stay again particularly taking into account the cleanliness issues and rats.
Great place to stay. Lots for the kids to do also. Caravan spot are nice and big.
Stayed for. couple of days over January and being greeted by an air conditioned room just turned my stay from great to outstanding.

Really disappointed with the economy 1 bedroom cabin (4 people).    Couch looked like it had been collected from the tip, cabin was so old and in need of basic maintenance/upkeep and some extra attention to detail cleaning. For the price we paid over 5 nights I was expecting alot more.
Bardzo dobre miejsce na nocleg. Łazienki czyste. Jest dostępna kuchnia, gdzie można sobie przygotować coś do jedzenia.

My family and i stayed in the safari deluxe glamping tent and it was fantastic, clean, bbq built in , a deck to sit on of an afternoon has a private bathroom and everything you would find in a basic kitchen , was a 15 minute walk to the beach ⛱️ close to shops very busy place hiring bikes would be perfect but we had a little baby  so couldnt we would go back
I wouldn’t go with the bunk bed setting, but everyone is different. We booked the two-room accomodation and it was perfect, with washing machine, dryer and dish washer.

I would like to express my frustration about my stay in Discovery Parks Byron Bay.

I booked an Economy 2 Bedroom Cabin - Sleeps 6, from Friday 15th Dec to Tuesday 19th Dec (4 nights) for a total amount of $768.

When I was finishing the check in, the receptionist quickly said I was being upgraded to a Delux Safari Tent, but I nobody asked me if I wanted that “upgrade”.
First of all, we are friends travelling together and we really wanted the 2 bedrooms and in the tent there were only curtains to separate the beds.
Secondly, there was a whole in the tent were spiders come in during the nights, we tried to closed the zip but it was impossible. See picture attached.
In addition, all the towels had wholes which makes me think that housekeeping is not even changing and checking towels, very disgusting.
Finally, we used the bbq the first night but we couldn’t use it more nights because there weren’t anything to clean it!!

To be honest, 768$ for sleeping 4 nights in a tent with wholes it’s TOO EXPENSIVE and abusive.

Rooms are good, cleaning average as all the wash basins gets clogged every time.

Worst no WiFi services at all, disaster when you are 500 dollars a day

The park has plenty to offer. Kids friendly water park, pool, BBQ, scooter and bikes for hire, basketball and lawn tennis court, bouncing pillow, amenities....clean rooms
Stayed for a few days with friends and kids, and just loved it there.
Toilets reeked of stale urine and walls were slimy. Staff only communicated via text messages. 1 of the pools was really dirty but the waterpark was fun for the kids.
Best staff and amazing water park for the kids
Discovery at Byron has been awesome for the kids with the water park and incredible for adults with the included Yoga class. Thanks to the teacher Glenys

Horrible. No park rules about noise, and no time limits for people to stop making noise. Loud obnoxious drunks are allowed to make as much noise as they want all night long. Paid a lot to stay here and got no peace or sleep. They just kept getting louder and louder with every passing hour. Unbelievably bad. No management care or responsibility whatsoever.

Response to owner's response:
The pamphlet of rules we were given made no mention of a curfew. Presumably our neighbours were given the same pamphlet with no mention of a curfew or any noise rules, and as a result thought they were at liberty to shout and sing at each other until 3am. The pamphlet also did not mention that staff are on site or that any monitoring would be done, and there was certainly no monitoring done while we were there.

We stayed here a few times and the kids love it. I will say though it is overpriced for how old the cabins are. Our cabin was overrun with ants and other insects and overall could use some love and care.
Not a bad  spot, but if you're unlucky enough to be put near the toilet/shower block then constant banging of the doors is very annoying all day and night. Needs to be looked at and changed.

The worst experience there. We booked for two weeks there and after 1 night the called me saying we had to leave in less than 24h because of the schoolies.
The knew that when we did the check-in (the night before) and they didn’t say anything.
Also, they didn’t give us any solution.
I’d never recommend this camping to anyone else.

Never got to stay unfortunately! Booked me in for a 2 night stay all paid for then 4 days before my arrival date decide they suddenly can’t accommodate me leave me and voicemail and just send me an email saying stay is cancelled! Very disappointing and unprofessional now we are left with nowhere to stay as everywhere is booked or ridiculously expensive

Awful experience. After, we made the booking with two weeks in advance and moved all our stuff just two days ago, the Discovery company told us we couldn't stay here longer because they booked the entire park to the schoolies.They didn't offer any help or solution, they didn't even think about our integrity because we don't have another place to stay the next following days.

The park is really good, location also but honestly I’m asking myself if there is maintenance or cleaning here. Amenities are so dirty, the walls of the showers are so dirty, two toilets are locked since days because of sludge in the ground, also all of the clogged sinks, and one of the pee toilet is full as… then all the leafs in the pool plus just one BBQ is working in the camp kitchen…
As I said the park looks really good, but that is not possible when you pay that amount. If it was cleaned I probably put 5 stars.
We had a fantastic time, and the location is great. Highly recommend 👌 👍 👏
Definitely stay again, cabins ok. Kid-friendly, nice park.
Amazing location, fabulous facilities. Couldn't recommend highly enough

Wir waren vier Nächte auf dem Discovery Park. Durch die etwas abgelegene Lage, war der Stellplatz dennoch ziemlich teuer. Die WCs und Duschen waren ziemlich dreckig. Wir haben während den vier Tagen nur eine Reinigungskraft gesehen. Sie scheint verständlicherweise kaum nachzukommen. Die Abfallcontainer waren überfüllt und die Vögel trugen den Abfall auf unser Stellplatz (siehe Fotos). Nach ausdrücklicher Bitte an der Reception haben sie die Kübel nach 3 Tagen geleert. Wir würden den Campground nicht weiterempfehlen.

Camping à un peu plus d'une vingtaine de minutes à pied du centre. Aménagements et équipements modernes avec une piscine, toboggan et matelas trampoline qui font la joie des enfants. Réception avec un accueil très sympathique.

We had a pretty ordinary experience with Discovery Byron Bay.

On arrival there was a mix up with the booking with the staff believing we were checking in the day after we arrived.  Without an apology we were told that we were getting put in an upgraded room after a bit of mucking about. Instead, we were put in a room on a corner. After being a bit disappointed with the accommodation we investigated online and discovered the room we were put in was not an upgrade and was in fact far cheaper than our original booking! I went to talk to the front office about this and asked to be moved to our original booking. I was told again that I had an upgrade and had to tell them that a cheaper room is not an upgrade and I also suggested that I don't think we should be paying for that nights accommodation, or at the very least the difference be refunded. They agreed to the latter reluctantly and let us know the next day when we could move to the new accommodation. The room was nice but the smoke alarm went off four times for absolutely no reason including twice in the middle of the night. There were also a nest of 1cm ants that have made a home in the shower area on the ceiling, fine but a bit gross however  I didn't love having to wipe away half a dozen ants from the toilet bowl each morning before sitting down.

Won't be staying again and would not recommend.

Stay in a modern cottage overlooking a pond, which had lily pads and ducks. Very comfortable.  Quiet and well appointed.  The water park looked like it would be great fun, unfortunately the weather prevented us from trying it out.

Pretty run down camp ground. But it does the job. Wifi was woeful. Accommodation/cabin had issues that even after reporting didn't get fixed. It was somewhat affordable compared to other options, but I've air bnb'd *much* cheaper.

The park is quiet with large sites, though the sites needed a good mow along with the grounds within the pool. Articles of clothing left in pool area remained for 4 days. Does that mean no grounds people stepped foot in the pool  area in that time?  Overheard from a child in the pool after diving with goggles, "but it's murky". Pool was cloudy. No Wi-Fi even tv in kitchen had problems. Amenities were spotless but if all the showers were being used you ended standing in next doors water. Nice coffee out the front. This lovely park just needs more ground maintenance. Lovely setting just far enough away from town. Would return.
Best.

Nice park,great water park and pools etc for kids camp kitchens fantastic and clean,only down fall was toilet/ showers were so tiny,you couldnt walk past any1 and was constantly wet floors,dirty and lacking toilet paper.we had to drive to laundry as was only 1 and located opposite side of park.other than that quite an enjoyable stay.
We stayed in deluxe cabins overlooking the lake, cabin was super clean , bedding was great spacious nice deck good size .

Beautiful 😍
Looking for a holiday park suitable for kids?
This is it!

There are a variety of different options from tents to cabins.
And even though there are loads of families with kids it never gets loud or annoying at night time ☺️

No wifi as advertised on the website. But mobile reception is reasonable in the area.

Perfect place to stay for a variety of different types of holiday and closer to shops and attractions

Very busy for October long weekend which is to be expected. They were completely booked out. Jumping pillow was always packed. Waterpark is great but was a bit cold. The large pool seemed slightly heated. Nice shady spots in the large sites near the jumping pillow, backing onto the river. River is not fully fenced off though so be careful with little ones.

Stayed in the safari tent and had a great time with three young kids. The Safari tent are not very sound proof so were told to keep the noise down at one point, so just something to keep in mind if thinking about that option. Room easily got messy also and was not really any proper cleaning offered. There also is no beach access via park.
Stayed 2 nites in a budget cabin

Possibly the worst experience with a management team in my entire life. Was spoken down on, had $400 stolen from me and had their whole staff make up lies to be little me in the checking out phase
If you don’t want people to stay in the park THEN DONT OWN OR WORK AT A PARK
You cannot tell someone that they can’t wait in the park for a ride because it’s “your property” when I’m paying to stay there
I stayed there a few years ago and cannot get over the time I stayed there

Large campsite with all amenities available. Tennis court, several swimming pools... everything you need for a great stay with friends, family or friends... close to Byron bay. The communal areas are always clean. I would recommend
Lovely campsite, staff very helpful. Clean site and facilities. Fantastic for kids and all the family. Great value too. Can't rate room as we had our own van
No WiFi !!! It’s not good!
Very good experience in Australia.

Lugar tranquilo y seguro.
Las cabinas son muy cómodas con todos los servicios incluidos.
Solo deberían ser más cuidadosos cuando ingresan acampantes!! Dejan  cosas tiradas y arruinan el parque.
Nice camping with good facilities.
Great holiday park, we stayed here for a week. lovely stuff, nice facilities. Highly recommend 😃

We camped.

The amenities and camp kitchen were way too far away for the campers. No use of any kitchen tools, so you had to bring your own gear. No place to stop the car to bring your pots, pans, food etc. Big hassle walking to and fro the camp site.

Most washers and dryers were out of order. Our clothes got stuck in the washing machine, big hassle.

2/4 bbqs did not work. Toilets were out of toilet paper several times.

We did tell the receptionist about all these problems and all she said was thanks for letting us know.

I hate leaving bad reviews and I can assume that they are understaffed but we and everybody else still paid full price and expected full amenities to work.

Location is SUPER.
Not impressed with our stay here, booked a room with a lake view & wi-fi, no view and no wi-fi. Staff at reception were unhelpful and robotic.
Amazing place

Rode here on a motorcycle with a few friends, the cabin we stayed in was very spacious and clean and tidy. The Pizza they had there was the best pizza i have  ever had! well done lads. Great activities for the children.
Luvly place to stay.
Camping with rooftop tent. Used the kitchen and bathroom and it was very clean. Unfortunately the wifi was extremely bad.

Very fair price (25$) for an unpowered site very close to Byron Bay. Nice and clean amenities, friendly staff. Would have given a 4/5 but the awesome Yoga lesson (every Saturday, Sunday & Tuesday at 8.30am) made it a 5/5.
This is the place to stay when visiting Byron Bay.

Wonderful stay! The room was cute and comfortable the only thing was I had to sweep the floors when we first got in as there was sand already on the floors when we first came in and the stove top needed a good wipe. The park grounds itself was stunning and a beautiful walk around with a heated pool a tennis court and cool kids water park and big bounce pillow.
Well organised. Great friendly staff. Clean facilities. Grounds are really well maintained resort standard.
A little more in price for the delux premium cabins but def worth it if you cab afford.

Quick 3 night break staying in a cabin overlooking their internal lagoon. Beautiful outlook, cabin and environment. Loved how jumping pillow was open early enough to occupy our kids but still not disturbing anyone else. Beautiful, well maintained gardens and lots of separate BBQ areas with lovely seating and views. Highly recommend!
Awesome location, quick drive to Byron - Main beach. Rooms are clean and well kept. Key box for easy check out.
